summary: DeVault declares that Sway 1.0 will not support Nvidia's proprietary driver or EGLStreams, only the open-source nouveau driver. He argues that Nvidia has consistently refused to support standard Linux kernel APIs (KMS, DRM, GBM) that every other GPU vendor supports, instead shipping their own proprietary alternative (EGLStreams) that was already rejected by the Linux graphics community. He frames the issue as Nvidia's responsibility to support Linux standards, not compositor developers' responsibility to accommodate Nvidia's proprietary stack. He criticizes users who reward Nvidia's behavior by buying their hardware and then expecting open-source developers to work around it for free.
key_points: Sway's new wlroots library will drop Nvidia proprietary driver support entirely, supporting only the open-source nouveau driver
Every GPU vendor except Nvidia supports the standard Linux kernel graphics APIs (KMS, DRM, GBM), including Intel and AMD with open-source mainlined drivers
Nvidia shipped EGLStreams instead of GBM to keep their driver proprietary without cooperating with Linux developers, despite the concept being previously rejected by the community
The burden of compatibility belongs to hardware vendors supporting open standards, not open-source developers accommodating proprietary ones
Users who buy Nvidia hardware and then demand free support from open-source developers are rewarding Nvidia's anti-Linux behavior
money_quotes: Sway 1.0 is not going to support the Nvidia proprietary driver, EGLStreams, or any other proprietary graphics APIs.
Nvidia, on the other hand, have been fucking assholes and have treated Linux like utter shit for our entire relationship.
Without GBM, Nvidia does not support Wayland, and they were real pricks for making some announcement like they actually did.
It is not my fucking problem to support Nvidia, it's Nvidia's fucking problem to support me.
Even Broadcom, fucking Broadcom, supports the appropriate kernel APIs.
Nvidia users are shitty consumers and I don't even want them in my userbase.
Choose hardware that supports your software, not the other way around.
key_insight: Hardware vendors must support open Linux standards rather than expecting open-source developers to reverse-engineer compatibility with proprietary alternatives.
